category_title: Chamorro 5th-grade student with ADHD and literacy challenges
scenario_title: Reading comprehension and word recognition on unfamiliar topics
minimal_context: The student is reading a 4th-grade level passage aloud and attempting to retell the story to the researcher.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please read this passage about snowstorms out loud, and when you are finished, I will ask you to retell what you read.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Reads quickly) The snow fell on the t... table. The wind was very f... fast.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep reading until the end of the page.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Finishes reading) Done. I think I did good because a good reader is someone who knows how to pronounce every word.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Now, tell me everything you can remember about the snowstorm passage.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sighs in frustration) It was about snow. That's it. I don't know anything about this stuff, I just read the words.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please read this passage about snowstorms out loud, and when you are finished, I will ask you to retell what you read.

STUDENT: (Reads quickly) The snow fell on the t... table. The wind was very f... fast.

TUTOR: Keep reading until the end of the page.

STUDENT: (Finishes reading) Done. I think I did good because a good reader is someone who knows how to pronounce every word.

TUTOR: Now, tell me everything you can remember about the snowstorm passage.

STUDENT: (Sighs in frustration) It was about snow. That's it. I don't know anything about this stuff, I just read the words.

task.content: Completing the Qualitative Reading Inventory (QRI), specifically word lists and comprehension passages at the 4th-grade level, and answering both literal and inferential questions

context.environment: Informal reading inventory administered by the researcher
context.materials: Qualitative Reading Inventory (Leslie & Caldwell, 2010) word lists and passages

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When he encounters unknown words in the passages and topics for which he has little-to-no background knowledge
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Gets frustrated with word recognition and comprehension, relies on the initial letters to guess at the word, and produces retellings that are generally skeletal with only the barest of details
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Thinks of reading as correct pronunciation; responses bore no connection to reading as a meaning-making process

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked about his perception of reading during a literacy interview
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Describes a good reader as someone who knows how to pronounce every word
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Equates reading strictly with correct pronunciation rather than comprehension
related_quotes: knows how to pr onounce every word.
